title: Use VaM's real UIToggle bool value path and restore side-screen delta placement
summary: 0.6.46 corrects the demo3 toggle-state seam by using the official bool-backed UIToggle value field instead of the float-style slider path, and it restores the side control placement back to the working screen-delta transform so the side stacks stay on their own screens.
changes: Demo3 toggles now serialize their default state as bool-style Trigger.value strings (`true` or `false`).
Runtime demo scene readback now resolves shuffle and A-B toggle state through JSONStorableBool and updates them without firing callbacks.
Side-screen control placement is back on the screen-delta transform path instead of the local-copy experiment that piled the side stacks back into the middle.
reasoning: Official VaM UIToggleTrigger uses a JSONStorableBool named `value`, so the 0.6.45 float-style toggle readback was the wrong API and led to incorrect default checked state.

The 0.6.45 side transform pullback overcorrected into a local-copy path that put the side controls back on top of the middle screen, so this slice explicitly returns to the working delta-based placement.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Shuffle and A-B loop should no longer default to checked when they are inactive.
The left and right control stacks should again appear on their own screens instead of collapsing into the middle.
knownIssues: This slice fixes toggle readback only; it does not yet add interactive A-B enable/disable behavior to the checkbox.
The generated scene JSON is still more verbose than a VaM-saved scene.
